title: Senior Full-Stack Software Engineer - Golang & Flutter
client: Grux (early-stage B2B SaaS, US-based, globally distributed team, knowledge management system)
budget: $10-35/hr (client explicitly open to higher rates for experienced; bidding top-of-band $35/hr)
engagement_shape: full-time (40h/wk) with PT overlap; cross-trained on two products (Grux + Rook); long-term hire intent
client_hiring_history: Featured Job (Upwork promotion); expert tier; ongoing; worldwide with approved-region restriction
tech_stack: GCP services (Cloud Run, GKE, Pub/Sub, Cloud SQL, etc.)
description: Grux (early-stage B2B SaaS, US-based, global team) hiring Senior Full-Stack Software Engineer. Golang backend + Flutter frontend (with Dart/JS). Cloud-native on GCP. Cross-trained on two products (Grux + Rook). Full-time, >30 hrs/wk, >6 months, contract-to-hire. Requires PT (Pacific Time, US West Coast) overlap during 8a-5p PT. Must be in approved regions/time zones. Strong preference for applicants with GCP + Go + Flutter production experience. Mandatory: 5+ years cloud-native, strong Go, hands-on Flutter, GCP ops, fluent English, full-time PT overlap.
questions_to_answer: Q1 (required): Describe your last two roles as a developer, including important responsibilities and deliverables.
Q2 (required): Have you delivered on an application from start to finish? If not, describe a project you had significant ownership over.
Q3 (required): Are you comfortable working in PT (Pacific Time) zone?
Q4 (required): What is your experience with Flutter?
Q5 (required): What is your experience with GCP?

estimated_value.rate: $35/hr (top of $10-35 band; client explicitly open to higher for fit)
estimated_value.annual_revenue_at_40h: $35 * 40 * 52 = $72,800/yr (with potential conversion to salaried FTE at higher comp)

required_tz_overlap: 8a-5p Pacific Time (UTC-8 PST / UTC-7 PDT). Required full-time with PT business hours.
approved_regions: Not explicitly listed in JD; client says 'specific approved locations/time zones' but doesn't enumerate. Common approved: US, Canada, Mexico, LATAM (similar PT-friendly TZs), UK (overlap hours), parts of EU (with PT shift). Bangkok (UTC+7) = 16h ahead of PT (PST); 8a-5p PT = 00:00-09:00 BKK next day. NOT viable for synchronous PT overlap.
notes: STRONG TECH FIT BUT CRITICAL TZ CONFLICT. Tech stack: Go + Flutter + GCP is a deep technical match for senior full-stack work — but the JD requires 8a-5p Pacific Time synchronous overlap. Bangkok (UTC+7) is 15-16h ahead of PT. 8a-5p PT = midnight-9am BKK next day. Real PT-overlap options for BKK-based engineer: (a) work graveyard shift 00:00-09:00 BKK (unhealthy long-term), (b) relocate to PT zone (not feasible mid-engagement), (c) negotiate modified overlap (e.g., 4-6h of PT overlap instead of full 9h). JD explicitly says 'Must be available for full-time work with overlap during Pacific Time business hours' and 'Must be located in approved regions/time zones' — this is a HARD filter, not a soft preference. **Decision: low-confidence bid with explicit TZ disclosure in proposal.** Same pattern as Job-128/130/131. Bid $35/hr top-of-band. If client accepts modified TZ (e.g., 4-6h PT overlap with async work outside), win probability improves. If client insists on full 9h PT overlap, not a fit. Client posted 3 min ago, <5 proposals — open competition.
decision_rationale: Strong tech-stack fit (Go + Flutter + GCP is in our integration+AI adjacent territory; user has 20+ years production engineering including cloud-native work on Azure AKS and 3,000+ POS terminals 24/7). BUT critical TZ conflict — JD requires full-time 8a-5p PT overlap, Bangkok is 15-16h ahead, not viable for synchronous work. Bid at top-of-band $35/hr with explicit TZ disclosure in proposal. If client accepts modified overlap (4-6h PT + async outside), strong candidate. If client insists on full PT overlap, not a fit. <5 proposals in 3 min — open competition, worth the bid.

qualifier_breakdown.tech_stack: 20/25 — Go + Flutter + GCP is strong fit, but NOT core stack (user's primary is Python/FastAPI/Prefect/Dask, not Go/Flutter). JavaScript adjacent. Node.js alt acceptable.
qualifier_breakdown.budget: 8/25 — low band ceiling ($10-35/hr); client explicitly open to higher = maybe stretched at interview
qualifier_breakdown.scope: 10/20 — strong scope (cloud-native platform, two products, full-time, >6 months, contract-to-hire); long-term upside
qualifier_breakdown.hours: 10/15 — 30-40 hrs/wk, but PT-overlap constraint reduces practical availability from Bangkok
qualifier_breakdown.deadline: 10/10 — >6 months ongoing + contract-to-hire = strong recurring revenue
qualifier_breakdown.client_tone: 4/5 — clear, professional, well-defined role; 'approved regions' is ambiguous but not hostile

risk_overrides.skill_mismatch: medium — Go + Flutter NOT core stack but adjacent; Node.js alt is closer to user's JS exposure
risk_overrides.override_reason: Tech-stack fit is strong, but TZ requirement is hard blocker. Bid with explicit TZ disclosure; outcome depends on client's flexibility on overlap hours.
